Title: OsCESA9 conserved-site mutation leads to largely enhanced plant lodging resistance and biomass enzymatic saccharification by reducing cellulose DP and crystallinity in rice

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase (CESA) family; P-CR conserved region
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cellulose biosynthesis / secondary cell wall biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Secondary cell wall cellulose synthase complex
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes UDP-glucose into β-1,4-glucan chains for cellulose microfibril formation in secondary cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for secondary cell wall cellulose biosynthesis; not explicitly defined as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural support of cell walls; major determinant of biomass recalcitrance and lodging-related mechanical properties
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem and hull secondary cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in stems at heading stage; function emphasized in secondary cell wall-rich tissues including stem and hull
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field growth (2012–2014); Calcofluor treatment; CGA treatment; MG132 treatment; chemical pretreatments with NaOH, H2SO4, CaO for saccharification assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: P-CR conserved-site mutation in OsCESA9 reduces cellulose degree of polymerization and crystallinity, decreases cellulose content and secondary wall thickness, but enhances lodging resistance, biomass saccharification, ethanol yield, and biomass production with little penalty to grain yield
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; complementation with full-length wild-type CESA9 cDNA; cell wall composition analysis; XRD for crystallinity; viscometry for cellulose DP; AFM; inhibitor treatments (Calcofluor, CGA, MG132); Western blot; co-immunoprecipitation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Osfc16 was mapped to OsCESA9 and carries W481C/P482S substitutions at a fully conserved P-CR site. 2. Complementation with wild-type OsCESA9 restored mutant phenotypes, confirming causality. 3. The mutation reduced cellulose content, cellulose crystallinity, and high-DP cellulose in secondary cell walls. 4. The mutant showed improved lodging resistance, increased dry biomass, strongly enhanced saccharification, and higher ethanol productivity. 5. OsCESA9 mutation impaired CESA4/7/9 complex integrity and promoted proteasome-dependent degradation of CESA proteins.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for precise genome editing of conserved CESA sites to lower cellulose recalcitrance while maintaining agronomic performance; useful for breeding rice and other crops for biofuel feedstock and lodging resistance, but requires careful allele optimization to avoid severe growth defects
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Slightly reduced plant height; thinner secondary cell walls; reduced cellulose level; otherwise little effect on normal growth and grain yield reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The focal allele is a conserved-site mutant (W481C, P482S) identified as Osfc16. The study proposes that reduced complex lifetime causes earlier β-1,4-glucan chain termination, generating low-DP cellulose with lower crystallinity.
